title: The Falling Asleep in Belonging Meditation
subtitle: A nighttime meditation for settling into sleep as connection with the resting world
excerpt: A ten-minute nighttime meditation from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The practice uses the image of the resting world (the trees, the air, the quiet) to create the felt sense of being held rather than left behind. The aim is not to produce a particular state but to let the body notice it is part of a much larger exhale.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 27 night practice of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

content.text: A ten-minute nighttime meditation from Day 27 of a 30-day sleep intensive. The practice uses the image of the resting world to create the felt sense of belonging: not alone in the dark, but part of the same exhale that is happening everywhere else at exactly this moment.
New to meditation as a sleep practice? Read
Lie down. Let the weight of your body be held by the bed, not something you are managing, just something that is happening. One long breath out.

Bring your attention outside the room. Picture the street, the neighbourhood, the trees at the edge of whatever is near you. All of it is quiet. All of it has slowed. The grass isn't trying to grow faster tonight; it's in its rest cycle. The birds are in theirs. The air itself is cooler, slower.
You are not separate from that. You are part of the same system that is doing this right now, everywhere.

Let your breath slow to match the pace of what's outside. Not a breathing exercise, just a noticing. Your exhale is part of a larger exhale. When your body is still, it's not absent from the world; it's in the same rhythm the world is in.
There is nothing to protect against in the quiet. The quiet is the thing you are part of.
Stay with the image of the wider world at rest: the trees, the air, the dark sky. You are held inside that. You don't need to fall asleep, just stay in that belonging until sleep comes for you.
Adapted from the Day 27 night practice of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
